claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children that I'd like to ask about - Alex, who's 8 years old, and Blake, who's 20. We all live together here in Manchester. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for them?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me gather a bit more detail to assess eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

Let me start with Blake, who is 20 years old. Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, or young people aged 16-19 if they're in approved education or training.

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training (such as full-time non-advanced education, A-levels, or an approved training course)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished their studies a while back and are just working part-time at a local shop now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that about Blake.

Now, regarding Alex who is 8 years old - just to confirm a few details:

**Do you live with Alex, and are you responsible for their day-to-day care (such as providing food, clothes, and their general upbringing)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely! Alex lives with me and I'm fully responsible for everything - their meals, clothes, school things, all of that. They're with me all the time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you currently living in the UK, and is anyone else already claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester as I mentioned. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them. It's just me looking after them both.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

**Good news for Alex!** ✓ You **are eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you're fully responsible for their care, you meet all the requirements.

**For Blake:** ✗ Unfortunately, you **are not eligible** for Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is 20 years old, and Child Benefit only covers young people aged 16-19 if they're in approved education or training. Since Blake has finished their studies and is working part-time, they're over the age limit for eligibility.

**What to do next:** You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex. Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, so make sure to claim for Alex to receive your payments.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age of 16. You live with Alex in the UK and are fully responsible for their day-to-day care, including providing food, clothes, and their general upbringing. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old. Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, or young people aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is not currently in any approved education or training - they finished their studies and are working part-time. Since Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, they are over the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with both children and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. Alex (8 years old) meets all eligibility criteria as they are under 16 and the claimant is responsible for their care. Blake (20 years old) does not meet the eligibility criteria as they are over the maximum age limit - Child Benefit only extends to those aged 16-19 if in approved education or training, and Blake is neither in that age range nor in approved education/training.
